text.title: Project references for monorepo incremental builds
text.summary: How `composite: true` and `references` make per-package builds depend on built `.d.ts`, not source.

section.title: Wiring references
section.p: Project references split a TypeScript workspace into independently buildable units. Each library declares `composite: true` and lists its dependents under `references`. Builds use `tsc --build` (or per-package `tsc -b`); editors get faster cross-package navigation because each package consumes its dependency's `.d.ts`, not its source.

rule.statement: Every library package's tsconfig sets `composite: true` and lists every internal dependency under `references: [{ path: "../<dep>" }]`. Application packages add references to all libraries they import. Run `tsc --build` from the root; never `tsc -p` per package in CI.
rule.rationale: Without `composite`, declaration emit is incremental-incompatible and editor navigation re-checks across boundaries. With it, each package builds once, downstream packages reuse `.d.ts`, total CI time drops 3–10x on large monorepos. `tsc --build` orchestrates the dependency graph; ad-hoc per-package builds skip the graph and rebuild redundantly.

example.note: `core` depends on `shared`; tsc --build will build shared first, emit .d.ts, then build core against those .d.ts.

antipattern.description: Importing across packages by relative path (`../../shared/src/foo.ts`) instead of by package name with a `references` entry.
antipattern.reason: Bypasses the project reference graph — tsc cannot determine build order, editors cannot do cross-package go-to-definition. Treat each package as an opaque module identifiable by its `package.json` `name`.

section.reference: moonrepo guide to TS project references.
